intro: Epidendrum quitense, also known as Quitense Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Andean region of South America, particularly in Ecuador. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Epidendrum quitense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is typically consumed as a tea or used in infusions, and it is also used in the preparation of herbal remedies such as syrups and tinctures.
